steps.title: Identify the cost driver
steps.content: HILDA's high cost stems directly from “years of data collection.” By contrast, the fitness study “may not need a large budget.” The blank must explain what differentiates the fitness study regarding this time requirement.

steps.title: Match the cost driver to the choices
steps.content:
D answers the blank directly: the fitness study “can be done well without years of data collection,” which explains the budget difference.

The other choices either suggest the fitness study should use longitudinal methods (A, reversing the logic) or focus on unrelated HILDA aspects like questions and sample size (B and C).

That confirms D is correct.
